steps.action: The Pentozali or Pentozalis (Greek: Πεντοζάλης) with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: The Pentozali or Pentozalis (Greek: Πεντοζάλης) with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It takes its name from the five (pente) attempt
steps.text: It takes its name from the five (pente) attempt or step (ζάλος zalos being a Cretan Greek word for "step").

steps.action: It can thus be translated as "five-steps".
steps.text: It can thus be translated as "five-steps".

steps.action: The name also contains an element of wordplay, a
steps.text: The name also contains an element of wordplay, as ‘ζάλη’ (zali) means dizziness, and so it may also be interpreted as a dance that can make its dancers dizzy five times over ("five-dizzy").

steps.action: In fact the dance has ten steps in total.
steps.text: In fact the dance has ten steps in total.

steps.action: The Cretan violinist Stefanos Triantafillakis (1
steps.text: The Cretan violinist Stefanos Triantafillakis (1715–1800) is credited with composing the music for the dance in 1770–1771.

steps.action: The Pentozali is a war dance, vigorous, with hig
steps.text: The Pentozali is a war dance, vigorous, with high jumping movements and allows for much improvisation.

steps.action: The dancers hold each other by the shoulders and
steps.text: The dancers hold each other by the shoulders and form an incomplete circle, which rotates counterclockwise very slowly, or sometimes not at all, because most of the lively steps are semi-stationary.

steps.action: Once the first dancer has finished their part, t
steps.text: Once the first dancer has finished their part, they are expected to break ranks and slowly dance their way towards the back of the line, yielding their place to the second dancer, and so on.

steps.action: This is thought to represent the line of success
steps.text: This is thought to represent the line of succession of command in battle, if the leader is killed.

steps.action: Both men and women perform the dance, but the st
steps.text: Both men and women perform the dance, but the steps of the women's dance were traditionally more restrained, because their long dress did not allow for high jumps.

steps.action: It was even thought that it was a dance sent fro
steps.text: It was even thought that it was a dance sent from God.

steps.action: It is the soloist who usually directs the flow o
steps.text: It is the soloist who usually directs the flow of the dance: he improvises to signal the first dancer to improvise too, and resumes the main tune when it is time for the first dancer to yield his place to another.

steps.action: Dances vary from island to island and it has bec
steps.text: Dances vary from island to island and it has become an everyday staple of Greek culture.

steps.action: It starts either at a fast pace or at a moderate
steps.text: It starts either at a fast pace or at a moderate pace and accelerates progressively.

steps.action: Before battle, the Cretans danced it in order to
steps.text: Before battle, the Cretans danced it in order to demoralize opposing armies.

steps.action: Every island has their own version and their own
steps.text: Every island has their own version and their own style.

steps.action: A version of it is also popular in the Dodecanes
steps.text: A version of it is also popular in the Dodecanese islands in the Aegean sea.
